video:video.video:title: Troy Baker stars in Netflix game 'Unhinged' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Troy Baker is an icon in the video game world, with lead roles as a voice and performance capture actor in "The Last of Us" and "BioShock Infinite" and villain turns in "Far Cry 4" and "Batman: Arkham Asylum."

video:video.video:title: Inside Wimbledon's queue, where fans wait hours for last-minute tickets
video:video.video:description: Wimbledon is one of the few major sporting events where fans can get tickets in person on the day of the match.

video:video.video:description: We asked children who taught them about money

video:video.video:title: Tips to talk about money with children
video:video.video:description: Personal finance expert Carrie Joy Grimes shares tips for parents to talk about money with their children

video:video.video:title: AP reporter witnesses phone call between Pope Leo XIV and his brother
video:video.video:description: AP reporter witnesses phone call between Pope Leo XIV and his brother

video:video.video:title: Pitbull fans break bald cap world record
video:video.video:description: Pitbull fans broke the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of people wearing bald caps. AP Entertainment Journalist Louise Dixon joined in on the fun.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire flames surround train in Canada
video:video.video:description: Ontario Parliament member Sol Mamakwa posted footage of thick wildfire flames surrounding a train crew in Canada to social media.

video:video.video:title: Trump warns 'godless communists' taking over Democratic party are 'most serious threat' to US
video:video.video:description: Seeking to energize his base ahead of November's midterm elections, President Donald Trump warned that "godless communists" taking over the Democratic Party pose "the most serious threat" to the U.S. since its founding as he addressed the Faith & Freedom Coalition.

video:video.video:title: Why does Trump still want Greenland?
video:video.video:description: Greenland has become one of the world's most strategically important places. Climate change, valuable mineral resources and growing geopolitical competition have put the island at the center of global security debates.

video:video.video:title: Funeral for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Najaf airport in Iraq
video:video.video:description: An official funeral ceremony was held Tuesday night for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Najaf International airport in Iraq.

video:video.video:title: Trump delays and rejects disaster aid for Democratic states at higher rates, AP finds
video:video.video:description: When major disasters strike, Americans are routinely waiting weeks — or even months — to receive presidential approval for aid.

video:video.video:title: U.S. military releases video it says shows strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it ended its latest wave of airstrikes targeting Iran. Iran retaliated with missile and drone fire targeting Bahrain and Kuwait before dawn.

video:video.video:title: Sightings of humpback whales surge in Rio de Janeiro, fueling demand for whale-watching trips
video:video.video:description: Sightings of humpback whales off Rio de Janeiro’s coast are surging as they recover from decimation due to commercial whaling, prompting an acceleration in the demand for whale-watching excursions to spot the huge marine creatures during their annual migration.

video:video.video:title: 'The building remains unstable,' New York mayor says about Manhattan building
video:video.video:description: A Manhattan high-rise under construction buckled and bricks tumbled into the street below; Mayor Mamdani says no injuries have been reported.

video:video.video:title: Iran releases video said to show latest strikes against US bases in Middle East
video:video.video:description: The U.S. launched strikes on Iran early Tuesday, hours after President Donald Trump vowed to reinstate an American blockade of Iranian ports and charge ships for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: US military says its latest wave of strikes targeting Iran is over and hit dozens of targets
video:video.video:description: US military says its latest wave of strikes targeting Iran is over, insisting Tehran does not control Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike overnight kills parents and their daughter in central Gaza
video:video.video:description: Mourners prayed and grieved for three Palestinians from the same family who were killed by an Israeli airstrike overnight into Wednesday, health authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Blackout hits Cuba as fuel reserve dwindles and aging grid crumbles
video:video.video:description: An island-wide blackout hit Cuba on Monday as fuel reserves dwindled and the electric grid continued to crumble.

video:video.video:title: Trump flies in an old Air Force One and transfers to a new one after Iran strikes
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has flown partway home from a NATO summit in Turkey on an old baby blue Air Force One plane instead of making the full trip aboard the new Qatari-gifted and retrofitted jet he arrived in.

video:video.video:title: Multiple runners injured on chaotic fifth day of Spain's San Fermin bull run festival in Pamplona
video:video.video:description: One runner was gored in the face and many more were fortunate to not be seriously injured during a chaotic bull run at Spain’s San Fermin festival on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Iran's president condemns Trump's 'rude language' against his country
video:video.video:description: Iran's president on Tuesday rejected U.S. President Donald's Trump "rude language" against his country as the U.S. launches further strikes in the region.

video:video.video:title: Senators deliver emotional tributes to Lindsey Graham after his sudden death
video:video.video:description: Sen. Lindsey Graham’s desk was covered in black cloth and a vase of white roses as the Senate opened Monday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: US Senator Lindsey Graham died after aorta tear, medical examiner says
video:video.video:description: The tear in the inner wall of the aorta, called an aortic dissection, was related to the hardening of Graham's arteries.

video:video.video:title: Sirens and explosions in Kuwait City as Iran and US escalate strikes
video:video.video:description: Kuwait announced on Saturday that its air defense systems had intercepted and downed Iranian missiles launched at the tiny Gulf State.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers at the scene after deadly landslide in China's Chongqing
video:video.video:description: A landslide Friday on the outskirts of the southwestern Chinese city of Chongqing caused deaths and left dozens missing, burying residential buildings and forcing more than 1,100 people to evacuate, according to local officials and state media reports.

video:video.video:title: Floor made entirely from peanut butter in honour of late Dutch artist

video:video.video:description: The official website of Iran's slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the country's state TV on Wednesday aired video purportedly showing a mosque in his residence compound after U.S-Israeli attacks in the war.

video:video.video:description: Today is a fairytale! Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce got married and AP was there.

video:video.video:description: Communities along the coastline of Nigeria are struggling to cope with heavy rainfall and unstable, sandy soils. Residents of the Orimedu community are living in fear.

video:video.video:description: China's Inner Mongolia is expanding both renewable energy and coal use. Despite the growth of solar and wind farms, coal remains essential.

video:video.video:description: The U.S. Justice Department and 17 states have reached settlement agreements with three major egg producers over allegations that the companies illegally colluded in a multiyear scheme to raises prices.

video:video.video:description: The storms came through Saturday downing trees and power lines, flooding several streets, and even causing at least one building to collapse, throwing bricks and debris into the street, crushing cars and blocking trolley tracks.

video:video.video:description: One of Spain's deadliest wildfires on record killed 12 people overnight into Friday as soaring temperatures grip much of the country.

video:video.video:description: Landslides triggered by heavy monsoon rains in southeastern Bangladesh have killed at least eight Rohingya refugees, including five children, while heavy monsoon rains battered parts of neighboring India, leaving 13 dead over the past few days.

video:video.video:description: Two people got to the top of the Empire State Building's antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” at midday Wednesday, before descending, embracing, taking selfies and ultimately being arrested.

video:video.video:description: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of a parasitic infection that causes severe diarrhea. Cyclospora spreads through contaminated food and water, often linked to fresh produce.

video:video.video:description: A federal officer shot and killed a driver during an immigration enforcement operation in Biddeford, Maine.

The summit comes as the alliance tries to persuade him that their countries are actively increasing their military capabilities even as the American focus shifts away from its defense of Europe toward Asia.

video:video.video:title: Emmy nominees describe how they reacted to their happy news

video:video.video:title: Matt Damon attended rowing camp for 'The Odyssey' preparation
video:video.video:description: 'The Odyssey' star Matt Damon says his role for the film was "not a standard acting job." (July 7)

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill at least five, Palestinian health officials say
video:video.video:description: Israeli strike in Gaza on Sunday killed at least five Palestinians and wounded over a dozen others, according to local health officials.

video:video.video:title: Security video shows deadly encounter with ICE in Biddeford, Maine
video:video.video:description: Footage from a security camera captured Monday's shooting during an immigration enforcement operation in the city of Biddeford, Maine.

video:video.video:title: Argentina reaches the World Cup final after 85 minutes of anguish in Buenos Aires and London
video:video.video:description: Football fans cried, sang and jumped in Buenos Aires and London on Wednesday after Argentina came from behind to defeat England 2-1 and reach the World Cup final for the second consecutive tournament.

video:video.video:title: Visitors reflect on America at 250 as they view original Declaration of Independence
video:video.video:description: Americans visiting the nation's capital are pondering the history and future of their country as they view an original founding document signed 250 years ago on July 4th, the Declaration of Independence.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors argue the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk should stand trial
video:video.video:description: The weeklong preliminary hearing marks the most significant presentation of evidence in the case so far.

video:video.video:title: King Charles has a pint to 'drown a few sorrows' after England's World Cup loss to Argentina
video:video.video:description: King Charles III said "maybe it's a good day to drown a few sorrows" as he poured a pint of beer at a pub a day after England's World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina.

video:video.video:title: US says it has ended strikes on Iran after hitting some 90 targets
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Thursday it had ended its latest round of airstrikes targeting Iran after hitting some 90 targets.

video:video.video:title: Truck carrying Khamenei's casket surrounded by crowd of mourners in Qom
video:video.video:description: Mourners dressed in black gathered just south of Qom in Iran for a for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral service, with throngs of people surrounding the truck carrying his coffin.

video:video.video:title: Toy Insider showcases holiday toy trends at Sweet Suite
video:video.video:description: Toy companies are offering an early preview of toys expected to top holiday wish lists at the annual Sweet Suite event, which showcases the latest games, collectibles, and must-have releases.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters try to contain fast-moving wildfire in Greece
video:video.video:description: Firefighters try to contain fast-moving wildfire in Greece

video:video.video:title: From Taylor's wedding dress to haute couture for Jonathan Anderson
video:video.video:description: Only days after Taylor Swift wore her Dior wedding dress, designer Jonathan Anderson showcased his new Dior haute couture show to stars like Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Ruth Negga and Naomi Watts on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Keanu Reeves finds humanity behind the horsepower
video:video.video:description: While promoting ‘Hooligans: The ARCH Racing Project,’ Keanu Reeves said his racing documentaries aren't just about speed.

video:video.video:title: Latin American governments prepare for El Nino as drought, floods and heat loom
video:video.video:description: Latin American governments prepare for El Nino as drought, floods and heat loom

video:video.video:title: Why is the internet obsessed with Erling Haaland?
video:video.video:description: Erling Haaland is one of soccer’s biggest stars, but it’s his goofy personality, viral moments and off-the-pitch charm that have made him an internet favorite.

video:video.video:title: U.S. Navy fighter jet flies low over beach
video:video.video:description: Watch the moment a jet from the Navy’s Blue Angels flew so low over a crowded beach in Pensacola on Wednesday that chairs and tents went flying.

video:video.video:title: Ten days after twin earthquakes, Venezuelans continue retrieving bodies of missing loved ones from rubble
video:video.video:description: Ten days after two powerful earthquakes shook Venezuela, families across the devastated north-central coast are still clawing through mountains of pulverized concrete with their bare hands to locate thousands of relatives missing in the ruins.

video:video.video:title: Anthony Michael Hall reflects on 50-year career
video:video.video:description: Anthony Michael Hall, known as a member of the iconic 80s "Brat Pack," found fame in films like "Sixteen Candles," "The Breakfast Club" and "National Lampoon's Vacation."

video:video.video:title: United by grief, mothers in Brazil demand reparations after police killed their sons
video:video.video:description: When a Brazilian police officer killed Ana Paula Oliveira’s 19-year-old son in a Rio de Janeiro favela in 2014, the mother of two didn’t think she would survive the grief.

video:video.video:title: Modi inaugurates India's first domestically built, hydrogen-powered train
video:video.video:description: The train will operate in the northern state of Haryana and can run at speeds of up to 75 kph (47 mph) and carry about 2,600 passengers, railway officials said.

video:video.video:title: Japanese beetles threaten Italian vineyards
video:video.video:description: An invasion of Japanese beetles is threatening Italy's vineyards. Swarms of the pests are munching through leaves, leaving grapes exposed and putting this year's harvest in danger.

video:video.video:title: Fans cheer as U.S. beats Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to keep its World Cup dreams alive
video:video.video:description: Folarin Balogun scored his third goal of the World Cup before being sent off with a red card in the second half, and Malik Tillman converted on a free kick to give the 10-man United States squad a 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina to advance to the round of 16.
video:video.video:tag: Bosnia and Herzegovina National Soccer Team

video:video.video:title: Indonesian president meets India's Modi in Jakarta for talks on expanding economic and defense ties
video:video.video:description: The visit marks Modi’s second trip to Indonesia as prime minister, following his previous visit in 2018, and comes after Prabowo attended India’s Republic Day celebrations as chief guest last year, Sugiono, who like many Indonesians uses a single name.

video:video.video:title: Workers at Ebola center in Congo strike over unpaid salaries
video:video.video:description: Dozens of people working at an Ebola treatment center in the Rwampara general hospital in northeast Democratic Republic of Congo went on strike Monday over unpaid salaries and bonuses, shutting down the hospital, blocking the road leading to the medical facility and burning tires in front of the mai

video:video.video:title: Dua Lipa opens a library in Portugal
video:video.video:description: Dua Lipa opens a library in Portugal

video:video.video:title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
video:video.video:description: There will be no turning back the clock if the House has its way. The House passed a bill Tuesday that would make daylight saving time permanent

video:video.video:title: Several found dead in lift after Brussels building fire
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out Tuesday at a building being renovated in downtown Brussels, injuring two people and leaving six missing.

video:video.video:description: Ten days after two earthquakes shook Venezuela, families across the devastated north-central coast are still clawing through mountains of pulverized concrete with their bare hands to locate thousands of relatives missing in the ruins.

video:video.video:title: China animal shelter owner takes great risks to evacuate dogs and cats from flooding
video:video.video:description: Mo Xingjian who runs a small animal shelter in the city of Nanning, wades through growing flood water from his home to rescue cats and dogs under his care.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verde soccer players given grand welcome on return home from first World Cup
video:video.video:description: With three draws, the Blue Sharks advanced to the round of 32, an unprecedented achievement for the small African nation, where they were defeated by Argentina in extra time.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire in eastern Spain forces evacuation of 500 residents from homes
video:video.video:description: A wildfire in Castellon, Valencia, forced evacuation of 500 residents from their homes. Regional authorities were battling a wildfire in Soneja in eastern Spain after it broke out Sunday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: Inside Israel's push to clear sea munitions as part of global push to protect waters
video:video.video:description: Israel's has launched a project aimed at clearing part of the seas to give beach space back to the population.

video:video.video:title: Sam Ryder: theater superstar
video:video.video:description: Sam Ryder is feeling the love at the opening night of his theatrical debut, as "Jesus Christ Superstar" at the London Palladium.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 13
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, July 13th: U.S. launches new strikes in Iran; U.S. and Iran claim control of Strait of Hormuz; Bangkok beer hall fire kills dozens; Aorta tear blamed for Senator Lindsey Graham’s death.

video:video.video:title: Moscow to 'respond in kind' to Ukraine with more powerful strikes, Putin says
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin on Monday said Moscow's response to Ukrainian attacks would always be "mirror-like" but "several times more powerful", he said as he toured an exhibition organised by the pro-Kremlin coalition All-Russia People's Front.

video:video.video:title: Three injured in Russian strike on Kramatorsk as Ukraine says it downs 130 drones
video:video.video:description: Three people were injured in a Russian airstrike on a residential area of Kramatorsk in Ukraine's Donetsk region.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy shakes up government, drawing protests across Ukraine against ouster of defence minister
video:video.video:description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy shook up his wartime government, drawing thousands into the streets Thursday across Ukraine to protest the ouster of his youthful defense minister, Mykhailo Fedorov.

video:video.video:title: Morocco fans in New York celebrate despite loss to France
video:video.video:description: Morocco’s World Cup run ended with a loss to France, but fans in New York continued to celebrate. France also eliminated Morocco at the 2022 World Cup, ending its historic run as the first African team to reach the semifinals.

video:video.video:title: Chicago area zoo helps animals cool off amid heatwave
video:video.video:description: Chicago area zookeepers put out ice treats and produce for bears and other animals in the midst of a summer heatwave. (AP video: Laura Bargfeld)

video:video.video:title: Former ship captain pleads guilty to all charges related to 2019 drugging and assault of student
video:video.video:description: John Merrone plead guilt to all five counts of an indictment charging him with aggravated sexual abuse, sexual abuse, and abusive sexual contact relating to the rape of a 21-year-old United States Merchant Marine Academy student cadet.

video:video.video:title: Puerto Ricans react to announcement of emergency water rationing as drought worsens
video:video.video:description: Water rationing measures were set to take effect in several communities in Puerto Rico on Friday as a drought grips the U.S. territory.

video:video.video:title: At least two people killed and five injured after Russia's overnight attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: At least two people were killed and five injured, including a child, following Russia's overnight missile attack on Kyiv on Thursday, Ukraine's Emergency Service said in a statement on Telegram.

video:video.video:title: Senate returns to Washington after Sen. Lindsey Graham's death with uncertain agenda, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans will return to Washington on Monday with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally with President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: View of destruction in Khan Younis, marking 1,000 days of war in Gaza
video:video.video:description: View of destruction in Khan Younis, marking 1,000 days of war in Gaza

video:video.video:title: Orbán allies protest in Hungary against plans to oust President Tamás Sulyok
video:video.video:description: Demonstrators opposing the Hungarian government's efforts to oust the country's president gathered in Budapest for a protest on Thursday after being called to action by the former autocratic prime minister, Viktor Orbán.

video:video.video:title: Oslo streets transformed into colourful gallery of graffiti art
video:video.video:description: Walls in one of Oslo's former industrial districts has become a giant canvas for street artists from around the world.

video:video.video:title: Martha Lillard, last US polio patient using iron lung, dies at 78 in Oklahoma
video:video.video:description: Martha Lillard had just turned 5 when she was diagnosed with polio and depended on an iron lung to live.

video:video.video:title: Lettuce at Taco Bell is linked to diarrhea-causing parasite outbreak in 5 states
video:video.video:description: Federal health officials have identified lettuce from Mexico served at Taco Bell locations across five U.S. states as a source of the widespread outbreak of diarrhea-causing parasite cyclospora.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 15
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, July 15th: US reimposes blockade, increases strikes as Iran threatens to halt energy exports.; Blanche faces questions in Senate confirmation hearing; wildfire smoke will engulf swaths of US; golden Mozart statues mark his 270th birthday.

video:video.video:title: Video shows huge fire at a Bangkok beer hall that killed 27
video:video.video:description: Authorities are still investigating the cause of the fire, but Bangkok Gov. Chadchart Sittipunt said investigators would examine the ceiling materials and whether any emergency exits may have been obstructed, potentially making it more difficult for people to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Hospital in France gears up for the next heat wave armed with lessons learnt from previous ones
video:video.video:description: At a Paris-region hospital, emergency medics needed it to plunge patients into cold-water baths to speedily bring down their temperatures so they wouldn't join the growing tally of dead from a record-smashing heat wave.

video:video.video:title: Fire in Bangkok pub kills at least 27 people
video:video.video:description: A fire which engulfed a beer hall in Bangkok overnight killed at least 27 people and injured dozens more. Firefighters brought the blaze under control early Monday.

video:video.video:title: A simple pair of glasses enhances productivity in some Bangladesh garment factories
video:video.video:description: In Bangladesh, home to the world’s second-largest garment industry after China, some factory owners are working on supplying more glasses to workers to boost productivity.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday July 8th: Trump says he thinks Iran interim agreement is over; Denmark’s Prime Minister says Denmark is ready to defend Greenland; Funeral procession in Iraq for late Iranian Supreme Leader; New York City building stabilized after collapse risk.

video:video.video:title: Trump seeks to enforce spending promises at NATO summit
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump heads to Ankara, Turkey, on Monday evening for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed alliance allies to spend more on defense.

video:video.video:title: Democrats grill Trump's pick for Director of National Intelligence on his role in elections
video:video.video:description: Senate Democrats aggressively questioned President Donald Trump's pick for a key intelligence post on how he would approach the issue of election interference ahead of the fall elections.

video:video.video:title: Mourners at Khamenei funeral procession in Tehran vow 'revenge'
video:video.video:description: A truck carrying Iranian late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's flag-draped coffin, crept through crowds of black-clad mourners in Tehran, who reached out to touch the grating.

video:video.video:title: Anti-immigration protesters march in South Africa
video:video.video:description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in parts of South Africa to rally against illegal immigration in the biggest migration-related protests since the wave of anti-migrant violence in 2008.

video:video.video:title: Why a traffic cone got a hero’s welcome in Boston
video:video.video:description: A traffic cone arrived in Boston on a first-class flight, complete with bagpipes and an official welcome.

video:video.video:title: Explosions wound at least 18 in Syria's capital during Macron visit
video:video.video:description: Explosions rocked the Syrian capital Damascus on Tuesday, wounding at least 18 people as French President Emmanuel Macron met with his Syrian counterpart in a landmark visit, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Fierce winds hit US Pacific territories as Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall near Guam
video:video.video:description: Super Typhoon Bavi made landfall on Monday, over a tiny U.S. territorial island in the western Pacific near Guam, bringing powerful winds and torrential rain to the Northern Mariana Islands.

video:video.video:title: Anxiety mounts for residents as Western wildfires continue
video:video.video:description: Residents across a number of states are facing uncertainty as wildfires spread across the West. (AP Video: Aya Diab)

video:video.video:title: Coffin of late Supreme Leader Khamenei paraded through Tehran
video:video.video:description: The coffin of late Supreme Leader Khamenei, and those of his family killed on Feb. 28 in an airstrike at the start of the war launched by Israel and the United States, were carried on a truck through a massive crowd of mourners in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Landslide in southwest China buries homes and forces hundreds to evacuate
video:video.video:description: A landslide Friday in the southwestern Chinese city of Chongqing buried residential buildings, forced more than 1,100 residents to evacuate and trapped an undetermined number of people, state media reported.

video:video.video:title: Memorial site grows for Maine motorist fatally shot by ICE
video:video.video:description: Community members stopped to leave flowers, signs and candles at a growing memorial for Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, a 25-year-old Colombian national fatally shot by ICE.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelan security guard pulled alive from building basement 8 days after twin quakes
video:video.video:description: Rescuers pulled a 43-year-old security guard alive from a collapsed basement early Thursday, ending a grueling days-long operation that became a symbol of hope after the devastation of twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela eight days earlier.

video:video.video:title: Trump and Infantino declare World Cup a success ahead of Argentina vs Spain final
video:video.video:description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino on Friday declared the World Cup a success, praising President Donald Trump for his role in the tournament and saying it had fulfilled its mission of bringing people together from around the world.

video:video.video:title: Brad Paisley says 'Tacklebox' honors classic country sound
video:video.video:description: Brad Paisley hopes 'Tacklebox' finds a home in fans’ playlists. He says the project captures the traditional country sound of the ’90s and early 2000s, and hopes it connects with listeners seeking that classic style.

video:video.video:title: Paraguayan Senate passes motion against Sen. Celeste Amarilla for remarks against Kylian Mbappé
video:video.video:description: The Paraguayan Senate passed a motion against Senator Celeste Amarilla after her racists remarks against France star Kylian Mbappé, while the Senator insulted the player again during the Senate session Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian fans and Japanese descendants celebrated a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over Japan
video:video.video:description: While emotions ran high in São Paulo's Liberdade district, celebrations took on a different scale in Rio de Janeiro, where Copacabana Beach became a sea of yellow and green after Gabriel Martinelli scored the winning goal deep into injury time, sealing Brazil's comeback victory.

video:video.video:title: 'We ain’t seen nothing yet,’ says ABBA's Björn Ulvaeus on AI-generated music
video:video.video:description: Björn Ulvaeus helped write some of the best-selling pop songs of the 20th century. Today, he's President of CISAC, the global body representing more than five million creators across music, film, literature and the arts, and one of the most active lobbyists on what AI owes the artists it's trained o

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of firefighters backed by helicopters and fixed-wing aircraft struggled Saturday to contain one of Spain’s deadliest wildfires that killed a least 12 people.

video:video.video:title: Australian officials ask fans to respect the privacy of Neil, a 1-ton seal who respects nothing
video:video.video:description: In June, a bellowing and blubbery 5-year-old elephant seal hauled himself onto land for his twice-yearly tour of beachside towns in southern Tasmania after months of feeding at sea.

video:video.video:title: Maine Democrat Graham Platner drops US Senate bid after woman's sexual assault allegation
video:video.video:description: Democrat Graham Platner has dropped out of the U.S. Senate race in Maine after a woman who dated him said he drunkenly forced her to have sex despite her telling him to stop.

video:video.video:title: Next steps after Graham Platner formally withdraws from Maine Senate race
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner has submitted paperwork to formally withdraw from the Maine Senate race, kicking off Democrats’ quest for a nominee they hope can defeat Republican Sen.

video:video.video:title: Trump's teleprompter operator on unpaid leave for alleged prediction market bets on Trump speeches
video:video.video:description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said she does not know of other White House employees accused of similar allegations, adding that the White House has “extremely strict, ethical guidelines with respect to issues like this.”

video:video.video:title: US-Russian crew blasts off for stint on space station
video:video.video:description: A U.S.-Russian crew successfully blasted off on a mission to the International Space Station on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Greece’s Mount Olympus vies for UNESCO world heritage site status
video:video.video:description: Mount Olympus, the mythological home of ancient Greece’s 12 Olympian gods, may soon join UNESCO’s World Heritage List.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, July 16th: Deadly flash flooding in Texas; Wildfires create hazardous air quality from Great Lakes to East Coast; Reports suggest White House teleprompter operator placed bets on Trump speeches; King Charles laments England's World Cup loss.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia surprises in a 9-year-old soccer player with July 4 World Cup tickets
video:video.video:description: Ben Snyder, a passionate young soccer player from Philadelphia, beamed with a radiant smile when he learned that he and his family would be attending the World Cup match on Independence Day.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 12
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, July 12: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies at 71; U.S.-Iran tensions rise after Strait of Hormuz attacks; Vietnam detains captain after deadly boat crash; McGregor’s UFC return ends with knee injury.

video:video.video:title: Mexicans resume daily life after another World Cup exit
video:video.video:description: Mexicans woke Monday to newspaper headlines marking the end of the national team's World Cup run after a 3-2 loss to England in the round of 16 ended hopes of a historic tournament on home soil.

video:video.video:title: Landslide in southwest China buries homes and forces hundreds to evacuate
video:video.video:description: A landslide Friday in the southwestern Chinese city of Chongqing buried residential buildings, forced more than 1,100 residents to evacuate and trapped an undetermined number of people, state media reported.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 1
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, July 1st: Lawyers for E. Jean Carroll demand $5 million from Trump; Supreme Court rejects Trump’s birthright citizenship limits; Democratic Socialist wins Colorado U.S.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military close entrances to West Bank town, disrupting residents' lives
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the town of Sinjil in the Israeli-occupied West Bank woke up Wednesday to find checkpoints to the village closed by Israeli troops, upending plans and disrupting daily lives and schedules.

video:video.video:title: Protesters hold walking vigil after ICE fatally shoots motorist in Maine
video:video.video:description: A federal immigration officer fatally shot a motorist in Maine on Monday. Dozens of demonstrators protesting ICE and President Donald Trump's immigration crackdown held a walking vigil in Biddeford that evening.

video:video.video:title: 10 killed after small plane crashes in the Bahamas, musicians among the victims
video:video.video:description: A small plane crashed in the Bahamas on Friday, killing 10 people and prompting the government to temporarily ground Flamingo Air flights.

video:video.video:title: Warsh says Fed has 'no tolerance' for high inflation but provides no hints on next move
video:video.video:description: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh pledged to make high inflation “a thing of the past” in his first congressional testimony Tuesday, yet provided no signal about the central bank’s next steps.

video:video.video:title: At least 5 children die in monsoon landslide at a Rohingya camp in Bangladesh
video:video.video:description: A landslide caused by monsoon rains killed at least five children as it swept through an Islamic school at a camp for Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh’s southeastern Cox’s Bazar district, a fire official said.

video:video.video:title: Three dead in about a week from ICE encounters
video:video.video:description: In roughly a week, three people have been killed related to encounters with ICE. This is what we know so far about the cases.

video:video.video:title: Cuba's public healthcare system in crisis amid U.S. embargo
video:video.video:description: After two surgeries and several rounds of radiation therapy over the past four years to treat a tumor, Irisleydis Tristá has spent the past seven months unable to get a CT scan to determine whether the cancer has grown or spread.

video:video.video:title: River Danube in Romania reaches record low flow in 30 years
video:video.video:description: The Danube River fell to its lowest flow in 30 years on Thursday and continued to drop through Saturday, with the downward trend expected to hold at least until Monday, Romania's National Waters Administration said.

video:video.video:title: Racist comments targeting France’s World Cup team spark backlash
video:video.video:description: Comments questioning the nationality of France’s players have drawn backlash from French officials and others, reigniting a debate over racism and representation in soccer.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, July 18th: US military says 2 troops are dead and 1 missing after Iran attacks; Ukrainian drones hit Russian warehouses; Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times; Argentina and Spanish fans ready for final.

video:video.video:title: 'Gilded Age' actors show support for Palestinian rights activist Mahmoud Khalil amid lawsuit filing
video:video.video:description: Khalil was joined Tuesday by his lawyers, fellow advocates and supporters, including 'The Gilded Age' actors Morgan Spector and Cynthia Nixon, at a press briefing in New York where they discussed the lawsuit and the ongoing fight for Palestinian rights.

video:video.video:title: Anya Taylor-Joy's World Cup 'stress'
video:video.video:description: Anya Taylor-Joy says the World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina has brought "a lot of stress" to her household because of her ties to both countries.

video:video.video:title: Supporters of Graham Platner react to his withdrawal from Maine's Senate race after new allegations
video:video.video:description: Dora Townsend and David Rice, a couple visiting Maine from Gettysburg, Pennsylvania, said they thought Democrat Graham Platner should drop from the state's Senate race after a new sexual assault allegation surfaced.

video:video.video:title: China's Xpeng debuts flying car in Germany
video:video.video:description: The Chinese manufacturer's 'Land Aircraft Carrier' carries a module in the back which can fly two people through the air. (AP video: Philipp Jenne)

video:video.video:title: Fans go full Pitbull in London for World Record attempt
video:video.video:description: Pitbull fans have broken a world record by dressing up as the music superstar. Ahead of Mr Worldwide's performance at BST Hyde Park on Friday, Guinness World Record officials counted the largest gathering of people wearing bald caps.

video:video.video:title: Compilation video shows Tyler Robinson on the day of Charlie Kirk's killing, investigator testifies
video:video.video:description: Tyler Robinson, the man charged with killing Charlie Kirk, strolled Utah Valley University in shorts and a T-shirt and bought a meal at Chick-fil-A before returning in different clothes to shoot the conservative activist from a rooftop, an investigator testified Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Eye and brainwave tech help artist with ALS find new stage as DJ
video:video.video:description: ALS, or amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, hasn’t stopped one artist in Tokyo from taking to the stage to perform with eye movements, AI and brain signals.

video:video.video:title: Stars at Schiaparelli as Haute Couture Fall/Winter begins
video:video.video:description: Emma Corrin, Michelle Yeoh and Bad Bunny are among the guests at the Schiaparelli Fall/Winter collection in Paris.

video:video.video:title: Residents face scenes of destruction as Russia launches a massive attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Russia has launched a massive drone and missile attack on Kyiv, killing at least 20 civilians in the city and injuring many more. (AP video shot by: Dan Bashakov)

video:video.video:title: At least two people killed and five injured after Russia's overnight attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: At least two people killed and five injured after Russia's overnight attack on Kyiv

video:video.video:title: Trump's envoy faces protests in Venice on latest stop of super yacht diplomacy tour
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Venice have clashed with police over the presence of a luxury yacht owned by U.S. ambassador Tilman Fertitta.

video:video.video:title: New England ice supplier describes joy and challenges of higher demand sparked by extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Extreme heat baking large parts of the U.S. generates extra business – and grueling working conditions – for New England ice vendor ahead of Fourth of July festivities in a hub of country's independence (AP Video and Production: Rodrique Ngowi.)

video:video.video:title: Hikes, helicopters and poisonous plants: behind-the-scenes on 'The Odyssey'
video:video.video:description: Zendaya and John Leguizamo chat about helicopter rides to Christopher Nolan's epic sets on "The Odyssey," and which plants to avoid.

video:video.video:title: US carries out new wave of strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: The U.S. carried out another wave of strikes on Iran as it reimposed a blockade, striking dozens of targets over seven hours, the U.S. military's Central Command said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Iran military releases footage it says shows missile launches against US forces
video:video.video:description: Iran's military released footage said to show missiles being launched in response to what it described as "U.S. aggressions and breach of commitments."

video:video.video:title: Much of America is about to swelter under climate-fueled heat dome
video:video.video:description: Most of America’s Lower 48 states are about to swelter under an unusually large, strong and long-lasting heat dome.

video:video.video:title: Spain and Gibraltar remove border fence in historic EU-UK treaty
video:video.video:description: The physical border between Gibraltar and Spain disappeared at midnight between Tuesday and Wednesday following years of post-Brexit wrangling over the contested territory of 40,000 people at the tip of the Iberian Peninsula.

video:video.video:title: Starmer chairs his last Cabinet meeting ahead of his last Prime Minister’s Questions
video:video.video:description: Cabinet ministers arrived at 10 Downing Street on Wednesday for the last meeting chaired by outgoing British Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

video:video.video:title: Alabama teen's shark attack inspires national alert system
video:video.video:description: Lulu Gribbin, who survived a near-fatal shark attack in 2024, has inspired federal legislation authorizing shark bite alerts.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 1
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, July 1st: Trump flies on new Air Force One gifted by Qatar; Data shows Trump made $1.2 billion from crypto; Ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz, Iran says; Traditionalist Catholics defy Pope Leo with bishop consecration.

video:video.video:title: 1 dead and 1 missing after pontoon boat fire near Alcatraz Island off San Francisco
video:video.video:description: San Francisco Fire Chief Dean Crispen said one person died and another was missing after a fire on a pontoon boat off San Francisco.

video:video.video:title: Boston welcomes traffic cone gift in celebration of Scottish camaraderie following World Cup pranks
video:video.video:description: The cone was welcomed as the latest chapter in Boston’s unlikely love affair with Scotland’s Tartan Army, whose habit of placing traffic cones atop statues during last month’s World Cup turned the cone into a symbol of the tournament’s spirit.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy warns Russia preparing new large-scale attack on Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Hours after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned on Sunday that Russia is going to launch a new large-scale attack on the country, Russia launched waves of missiles and drones targeting Kyiv overnight into Monday that killed at least seven people, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Indian Prime Minister Modi welcomed at Government House in New Zealand
video:video.video:description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi was welcomed at Government House in Auckland on Saturday with a traditional ceremony and a troop review.

video:video.video:title: Three strikes hit different zones of central Gaza after warning from the Israeli army
video:video.video:description: Three strikes hit central Gaza on Wednesday evening after the Israeli military issued a warning, according to area residents.

video:video.video:title: Police break up Tibet protest outside Chinese Embassy in India
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Tibetan Youth Congress activists protested outside the Chinese Embassy in New Delhi on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Whoopi Goldberg reflects on 'The Whoopi Monologues'
video:video.video:description: As "The Whoopi Monologues" gets an Off-Broadway revival featuring Kerry Washington, Whoopi Goldberg looks back at her inspirations. (July 13)

video:video.video:title: What to know about the outbreak of diarrhea-causing parasite, cyclospora
video:video.video:description: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of a parasitic infection that causes severe diarrhea. Nearly 1,000 people have been diagnosed with cyclospora as of Wednesday, though no deaths have been reported.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: FIFA lifts US star striker red card suspension at World Cup after Trump calls Infantino
video:video.video:description: Folarin Balogun received a red card for stepping awkwardly on the right ankle of Tarik Muharemović of Bosnia-Herzegovina in a 2-0 round of 32 win on Wednesday, triggering an automatic one-game suspension.

video:video.video:title: Mexican man killed in Houston ICE shooting was not the target of operation, lawmaker says
video:video.video:description: A U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican national who lived in the U.S. for decades, as the homebuilder drove his construction crew to a Houston job site.

video:video.video:title: Starmer says Farage is calling by-election because he is 'up to his neck in sleaze'
video:video.video:description: "This is a desperate stunt from Nigel Farage, and it's obvious why he's doing it. He is up to his neck in sleaze," UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said in Ankara, where he is attending a NATO summit.

video:video.video:title: AP finds that Trump delays and rejects disaster aid for Democratic states at higher rates
video:video.video:description: When major disasters strike, Americans are routinely waiting weeks — or even months — to receive presidential approval for aid.

video:video.video:title: Women are dying in Africa as US ramps up its global battle against abortion
video:video.video:description: Women are dying in Africa as US ramps up its global battle against abortion

video:video.video:title: Indigenous women in Bolivia take their traditional attire to skateboarding event
video:video.video:description: A group of Bolivian women and girls wore their traditional Indigenous attire in La Paz on Tuesday as they competed in a skateboarding youth talent show.

video:video.video:title: Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times after destroying more than 100 homes
video:video.video:description: The largest residential fire in modern Norwegian history destroyed more than 100 homes and forced hundreds of people to evacuate, authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Who is Andy Burnham? The man who went from Manchester mayor to Britain's next prime minister
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham got to the top through a mix of patience and risk-taking. A decade ago, Burnham abandoned a 20-year climb up the Labour Party ladder in London to head north and run for mayor of Greater Manchester.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry's UK trip sparks media buzz over whether Meghan and kids will join him
video:video.video:description: The drama that seems to surround Prince Harry returns to the U.K. this week and the previews already have the British press buzzing with anticipation.

video:video.video:title: Man held over murder of wife and daughters in UK appears in South Africa court
video:video.video:description: Triple murder suspect Ndodana Mkhanyisi Tshuma, wanted by British authorities on suspicion of killing his wife and two young daughters in England, appeared in a Johannesburg court on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese animal shelter takes in pets abandoned by war, displaced by Israeli invasion
video:video.video:description: In the village of Kafroue in southern Lebanon, Hussein Hamza makes his daily rounds to feed and check on his furry and feathered charges.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire smoke blankets Toronto, raising air quality concerns
video:video.video:description: Smoke from wildfires blanketed Toronto on Wednesday, creating hazy skies and prompting health concerns as residents faced poor air quality, according to CTV.

video:video.video:title: France soccer fans celebrate 2-0 victory at World Cup, Morocco supporters heartbroken after loss
video:video.video:description: Jubilant France soccer fans celebrated outside the stadium after their team won the first quarterfinal match of the 2026 World Cup against Morocco.

video:video.video:title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz appears before Senate committee
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration's latest nominee to lead the nation' top public health agency drew frustrated reactions from some U.S. senators on Wednesday when they pressed her on whether she would protect the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention from political meddling.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Hemingway’s masterpiece on Spain’s bull runs turns 100 years old
video:video.video:description: This year’s San Fermin festival in Spain marked the 100th anniversary of the publication of Ernest Hemingway’s book “The Sun Also Rises” that launched the future Nobel Laureate to literary fame and put Pamplona on the map for millions of people around the world.

video:video.video:title: Child among several Palestinians killed by Israeli fire and bombings in Gaza
video:video.video:description: At least nine Palestinians were killed by Israeli fire and airstrikes in the war-torn Gaza Strip, health authorities said on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Multiple blasts as Yemen airport hit in airstrikes
video:video.video:description: Multiple blasts were seen at Sanaa airport in Yemen on Monday in what Iran-backed Houthi rebels said were Saudi airstrikes while the internationally recognized government in Yemen said they were meant to prevent an Iranian plane from landing.

video:video.video:title: Katherine LaNasa reacts to Emmy nom for 'The Pitt'
video:video.video:description: Katherine LaNasa says her Emmy nomination is a "huge compliment." She was nominated for outstanding supporting actress in a drama series for her role in "The Pitt."

video:video.video:title: Russian attack kills at least 20 and causes damage across Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Loud explosions shook the Ukrainian capital for hours during the night, with many people sheltering in subway stations after authorities issued air raid warnings.

video:video.video:title: Indian Prime Minister welcomes Japanese Prime Minister to New Delhi
video:video.video:description: Japan's leader was in India to meet with the country's prime minister. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi was greeted in New Delhi by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

video:video.video:title: Drone show lights up night sky over Philadelphia's Independence Hall
video:video.video:description: Philadelphia held a drone show outside Independence Hall to highlight the upcoming Fourth of July weekend in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of Hezbollah supporters in Beirut vow loyalty to late Iranian leader Khamenei
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Hezbollah supporters dressed in black gathered in Beirut's southern suburbs, waving Hezbollah and Iranian flags, expressing loyalty to Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes scene on the street near Taylor Swift's wedding
video:video.video:description: Fans lined the streets of New York Friday, looking to catch a glimpse of guests heading in to the wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden wedding.

video:video.video:title: Thai zoo celebrates famous pygmy hippo's second birthday
video:video.video:description: A zoo in Thailand celebrated the 2nd birthday of the viral sensation pygmy hippo Moo Deng on Friday, with a large cake made of fruits and vegetables.

video:video.video:title: Venezuela earthquake survivors face water shortages, receive aid packages
video:video.video:description: Venezuelans affected by the devastating earthquakes last month received some aid on Wednesday but increasingly face challenges accessing clean water as well as sanitation and hygiene services.

video:video.video:title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer answers questions from lawmakers for the last time as PM
video:video.video:description: UK PM Keir Starmer, who leaves office next week, bid farewell to the boisterous weekly Prime Minister’s Questions sessions where he has traded barbs with opposition politicians and defended his government’s record.

video:video.video:title: Trump says FBI 'wasting their time' on probing Sen. Graham's death
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the FBI is “wasting their time” if it is investigating the death of Sen.

video:video.video:title: Scientists collect data behind forecasts at UK weather station, as country swelters in latest heatwave
video:video.video:description: Weather stations turn heat, wind, rain and sunshine into the numbers behind forecasts, records and climate reports.

video:video.video:title: Vance says small, but loud voices will speak of America's imperfections on its birthday
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance spoke aboard the USS Kearsarge in New York Harbor on July 4th and said some small but loud voices would speak "obsessively" on America's birthday about its imperfections instead of its greatness.

video:video.video:title: Fans of K-pop supergroup BTS march in Chile over venue uncertainty
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans of South Korean group BTS marched through downtown Santiago on Sunday to demand confirmation of the venue for the band’s upcoming performances.

video:video.video:title: Kentucky farmer adapts harvest, protects crops during extreme heat
video:video.video:description: The recent heat dome affected some specialty farmers who produce crops like fruits and vegetables. Intense heat is the result of a human-driven climate change pattern of weather extremes that may lead to loss of crops for farmers and forces them to adapt their practices.

video:video.video:title: When is the best time to buy a house? Business basics: mortgage rates
video:video.video:description: Mortgage rates play a big role in what you'll pay each month for your home. But with rates constantly changing, waiting for the "perfect" rate could mean missing the right opportunity to buy.

video:video.video:title: From deep-sea snails to desert frogs - latest Red List shows no ecosystem is safe from human industry
video:video.video:description: From deserts to the deep ocean, hundreds of thousands of species are at risk according to the latest IUCN report.

video:video.video:title: Delve into the secret lying underneath Japan's ancient Asuka-Fujiwara sites
video:video.video:description: The ancient Japanese capitals of Asuka and Fujiwara have been submitted to UNESCO as one of the nominations for this year's new World Heritage Sites.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters deployed across Paris region forest to fight exceptional fire
video:video.video:description: It was among several wildfires in western Europe as the region bakes under its third red-alert heat wave this year.

video:video.video:title: Judge orders E. Jean Carroll be paid $5.6M in Trump sex abuse and defamation case
video:video.video:description: Writer E. Jean Carroll can collect over $5.6 million held in escrow since a jury found that President Donald Trump sexually abused and defamed her, a federal judge ruled.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: A 12-year-old baseball prodigy trains through tears after Venezuela quakes leave him homeless
video:video.video:description: At what was once his baseball field, 12-year-old Yeferson Seijas now lives with his family in a makeshift tent after twin earthquakes devastated Venezuela's coastal state of La Guaira.

video:video.video:title: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78
video:video.video:description: Actor Sam Neill's death was “sudden and unexpected,” a family statement said, adding that he “remained cancer free” when he died. A cause of death wasn’t specified.

video:video.video:title: South Carolina residents reflect following the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham
video:video.video:description: Residents of Fort Mill, South Carolina, react to the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham, who served in Congress for more than three decades, had turned 71 years old just two days before dying on Saturday night.

video:video.video:title: Rome prepares for heatwaves by helping elderly people who live alone
video:video.video:description: The project, called “Viva gli anziani” (“Long Live the Elderly”), aims to assist older people not only by bringing them groceries and providing financial help, but also by building a social community around them.

video:video.video:title: How the Strait of Hormuz is monitored as US-Iran conflict escalates
video:video.video:description: The United States intensified its strikes on Iran early Thursday, hitting targets further north as American forces also fired into a ship the U.S. accused of trying to break its naval blockade on the Islamic Republic.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Maine demand answers after ICE shoots Colombian man
video:video.video:description: Immigrant rights groups are demanding answers and planning more protests Tuesday over an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer's fatal shooting of a Maine driver.

video:video.video:title: Japan space agency's reusable rocket lands safely in first test flight
video:video.video:description: The RV-X rocket lifted off, hovered and moved horizontally before landing during its less than one-minute flight at the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency’s (JAXA) Noshiro Testing Center in northeastern Japan.

video:video.video:title: Trump reduces size of two national monuments in Utah
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Monday sharply reduced the size of two national monuments in Utah, undoing protections established by his Democratic predecessors on public lands that are sacred among many Native Americans.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US will give Ukraine license to make Patriot missiles to counter Russia
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the US will give a license to Ukraine to build Patriot air defense systems, a major boost for Kyiv as it battles Russia.

video:video.video:title: Onlookers react to banner atop Empire State Building
video:video.video:description: Two people got to the top of the Empire State Building’s antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” at midday Wednesday, before descending, embracing, taking selfies and ultimately being arrested.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strikes cause massive damage to areas of Gaza it told people to leave
video:video.video:description: Israel ordered Palestinians in four major areas of the central Gaza Strip to evacuate before intense airstrikes Wednesday night that residents said heavily damaged homes they had returned to in hopes of rebuilding their lives during a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday July 6th: Iran begins funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; Ukraine says Russia launched massive missile and drone strike; Deaths in New Jersey blamed on heat; FIFA lifts red card suspension for U.S. star player Folarin Balogun.
video:video.video:tag: Bosnia and Herzegovina National Soccer Team

video:video.video:title: Cuba suffers third outage of national power grid in less than two weeks
video:video.video:description: Cuba experienced another outage in the National Energy System (SEN) on Tuesday, leaving the entire country without power, according to the Ministry of Energy and Mines.

video:video.video:title: Tourists and New Yorkers face uncertainty after Manhattan high-rise scare
video:video.video:description: People evacuated from buildings near a Manhattan high-rise with buckled columns describe spending the night without a place to stay. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza watch World Cup clash between Egypt and Argentina
video:video.video:description: Egypt shares Gaza’s only Arab border through the Rafah crossing and has long served as one of the main mediators in ceasefire negotiations between Israel and Hamas, alongside Qatar, Turkey and the United States.

video:video.video:title: Ex-wife of ICE officer in Maine shooting tells AP he has history of violent behavior
video:video.video:description: The Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer who shot a Colombian man in Maine this week is an Army veteran who has struggled with serious mental health issues since early childhood, his ex-wife tells AP.

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate in Madrid as Spain shuts down France 2-0 to earn spot in World Cup final
video:video.video:description: Spanish fans celebrated in Madrid following Spain's 2-0 victory over France on Tuesday. In footage captured by the Associated Press, Spain fans could be seen taking to the streets of Madrid following the win, and later being dispersed by police.

video:video.video:title: Chinese men in Germany used Telegram to share rape videos and drugging tips
video:video.video:description: A German investigation into an online predator network that thrived on Telegram for years has led to the convictions of three alleged members of the group's inner circle and the ongoing trial of a fourth in Berlin.

video:video.video:title: UK urges FIFA to investigate Argentina over Falklands banner at World Cup
video:video.video:description: The British government on Thursday urged FIFA to investigate Argentina’s team after players posed with a banner claiming sovereignty over the contested Falkland Islands.

video:video.video:title: Bayeux Tapestry at the British Museum after secret journey from France
video:video.video:description: After almost 1,000 years, the Bayeux Tapestry is back on English soil. In scenes like a heist movie in reverse, the priceless Medieval artwork was spirited into the British Museum on Friday in the dead of night, after a high-tech, tight-security operation where any slip-up could have spelled disaste

video:video.video:title: Is Broadyway ready for Dwayne Johnson?
video:video.video:description: At the New York premiere of "Moana," Dwayne “The Rock” Johnson says he would love to do Broadway. Johnson says he had been previously working with Jeffrey Seller on a theatrical version of "The Odd Couple."

video:video.video:title: Reading glasses transform the work and lives of Bangladesh garment workers
video:video.video:description: Glasses are helping to boost productivity and quality of life for workers in some Bangladesh garment factories.

video:video.video:title: Tal Anderson celebrates Emmy nom for 'The Pitt'
video:video.video:description: Tal Anderson celebrates her first Emmy nomination for her role in "The Pitt." She says the nomination is especially meaningful because of the opportunity to portray a character with autism.

video:video.video:title: Le Pen's future as French presidential contender hinges on Paris appeals court ruling
video:video.video:description: Marine Le Pen and other members of her National Rally party were found guilty of using money intended for parliamentary assistants to pay party staff between 2004 and 2016.

video:video.video:description: Dramatic footage showed a man escaping from a fire at an apartment block on the outskirts of the Belgian city of Antwerp, climbing from a balcony to a nearby window.

video:video.video:title: Alzheimer's blood test may predict earliest stage of disease but doctors warn it lacks precision
video:video.video:description: A blood test may predict the start of Alzheimer's symptoms years in advance and doctors say it could become a game changer.

video:video.video:title: Tofu vendor delivers vital friendship alongside healthy food in Tokyo
video:video.video:description: A tofu seller’s pink cart has become a small defence against loneliness on the alley-like streets of east Tokyo.

video:video.video:title: Mbappé condemns Paraguyan senator over racist remarks after World Cup match
video:video.video:description: France star Kylian Mbappé condemned Celeste Amarilla, a Paraguayan senator, over racist remarks she made following Paraguay’s loss to France in the round of 16 at the World Cup.

video:video.video:description: Argentina fans gathered in front of the Atlanta stadium to celebrate their semifinal win while England fans left disappointed. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:description: Although electric tricycles are common in many countries, in Cuba they are now the primary mode of transportation due to a severe fuel shortage caused by a U.S blockade.

video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, July 14th: Protests in Maine following ICE shooting; Iran retaliates for latest U.S. airstrikes; Houthi rebels in Yemen blame Saudi Arabia for attack on airport; Lindsey Graham’s sister picked to temporarily replace him in Senate.

video:video.video:title: US attacks Iran as Tehran retaliates against UAE tankers in Strait of Hormuz and Bahrain
video:video.video:description: The U.S. launched strikes on Iran early Tuesday morning, hours after President Donald Trump said Washington is “reinstating” a blockade on Iran in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: What a permanent switch to daylight saving time could mean for you
video:video.video:description: Daylight saving time is that period between spring and fall when clocks in most parts of the United States are set one hour ahead of standard time.

video:video.video:title: Maine Democrat Graham Platner in a social media post says he plans to withdraw from Senate race
video:video.video:description: Democrat Graham Platner says he plans to withdraw from the U.S. Senate race in Maine after a woman who dated him said he drunkenly forced her to have sex despite her telling him to stop.

video:video.video:title: Riding through history on Switzerland's Furka steam railway
video:video.video:description: Train enthusiasts will celebrate the 100th anniversary of a historic Swiss railway this weekend as vintage steam locomotives ferry tourists through the stunning Alpine scenery as part of the celebration.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans in Buenos Aires jubilant as team beats Switzerland to reach World Cup semifinals
video:video.video:description: Jubilant Argentinian fans in Buenos Aires celebrated into the early hours of the morning after their country beat Switzerland in the World Cup to advance to the semifinals of the tournament.

video:video.video:description: As this year's Dark Mofo art festival came to an end, the Museum of Old and New Art (MONA) in Hobart unveiled its largest expansion since opening 15 years ago.

video:video.video:description: Settler violence surges across the West Bank

video:video.video:description: Kyle Bylin and Jeremy Morrison were both born on Jan. 26, 1988, at Unity Medical Center in Grafton, North Dakota.

video:video.video:title: Spain and Gibraltar remove border fence in a historic EU-UK treaty
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people who travel every day between the southern tip of Spain and the British territory of Gibraltar will no longer have to cross a physical border beginning Wednesday after a border fence was fully removed, allowing a new freedom of movement under a historic European Union-U.K. treaty

video:video.video:title: Father finds missing daughter under rubble as Venezuelans unearth loved ones without state help
video:video.video:description: After 11 days of using just his hands to dig through the muck and rubble of the twin earthquakes that devastated his public housing development in northern Venezuela, Alexander, dazed and sleep-deprived, reached the last missing member of his family member on Sunday — his 12-year-old daughter.

video:video.video:description: Italy on Tuesday put on display one of the best known examples of Etruscan painting, from a tomb that has become the latest acquisition in the Culture Ministry’s buying spree of big-ticket pieces of the country’s cultural heritage.

video:video.video:description: Scientists built a flapping wing robot that can swim underwater, fly through the air and launch itself out of water to study how diving birds manage the same challenge.

video:video.video:description: “Our goal is to build a shared ballistic missile defense capability for Europe,” the 10 nations said in a statement in Paris at talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

video:video.video:title: Tom Holland talks working alongside 'The Odyssey' cast
video:video.video:description: Tom Holland says "The Odyssey" sets held "a treasure trove of lessons" for him and Zendaya.

video:video.video:description: Greenpeace Italy and Italian main labour union CGIL installed three ice statues, representing a farmer, a bicycle rider and a construction worker, and left them to melt under the scorching sun in front of the Colosseum on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration silent on Iranian school strike four months later
video:video.video:description: It was the deadliest reported strike in the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, and most of the victims were children.

video:video.video:description: Ernest Hemingway's 1926 novel 'Fiesta: The Sun Also Rises' captivated generations of readers with its story of exotic travel and the pursuit of impossible love.

video:video.video:description: The unexpected resignation is an effort by the anti-immigration politician to preempt a standards investigation into undeclared and potentially rule-breaking donations that could have seen him ejected as a lawmaker, and to present himself as the victim of a witch hunt by the media and his political

video:video.video:description: Khamenei's sons Masoud, Meysam and Mostafa, as well as Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf and Revolutionary Guard head Gen.

video:video.video:title: Federal Reserve Chair Warsh emphasizes political independence, signals focus on inflation
video:video.video:description: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that the central bank would remain independent and seek to bring down inflation, likely foreclosing the rate cuts President Donald Trump has sought.

video:video.video:title: Senators deliver emotional tributes to Lindsey Graham after his sudden death
video:video.video:description: Senate Republicans returned to Washington on Monday with an uncertain agenda after the sudden death of prominent Republican Lindsey Graham, a committee chairman and key player who served as a crucial ally of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors argue the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk should stand trial
video:video.video:description: A five-day preliminary hearing for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk is underway in Utah.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 16
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, July 16th: U.S. intensifies strikes on Iran; Trump says ICE should keep making vehicle stops; Flooding in South Texas; Argentina going to World Cup finals.

video:video.video:title: Former UK MP Ann Widdecombe killed in 'targeted attack' say police
video:video.video:description: Former politician and reality TV contestant Ann Widdecombe was killed in a “targeted attack,” though the motivation is still under investigation, British counterterror police said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Sirens sound in Kuwait after it says its air defences intercepted incoming fire
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded in Kuwait on Wednesday as the country's air defenses engaged to intercept incoming fire after the United States launched airstrikes targeting Iran.
