video:video.video:title: 1 dead and 3 missing after boat carrying mostly family members sinks near Alcatraz Island
video:video.video:description: One person died, three people were missing and 16 were rescued from the waters off San Francisco after a pontoon boat sank Tuesday afternoon while carrying mostly family members as part of a memorial service, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Bonnie Tyler on her music industry success
video:video.video:description: Bonnie Tyler, the Grammy-nominated Welsh pop star best known for singing the chart-topping power ballad “Total Eclipse of the Heart” has died at 75.

video:video.video:title: From a little house to a bigger stage, Motown Museum readies its $75M expansion
video:video.video:description: A $75 million expansion of the Motown Museum in Detroit is entering its final phases ahead of a grand opening next year. (AP video: Mike Householder and Corey Williams)

video:video.video:title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
video:video.video:description: Proponents, including the White House, argued the change would provide more daylight during the times that Americans are most active.

video:video.video:title: French Far-right leader Marine Le Pen plans to run for president with court-ordered ankle tag
video:video.video:description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen was sentenced Tuesday to wear a court-ordered electronic monitor after a conviction for embezzlement.

video:video.video:title: Fighter jet flies low over a crowded beach in Florida
video:video.video:description: In the latest manuever, video spreading widely on social media shows a jet from the Navy's demonstration squadron, the Blue Angels, flying so low over a crowded beach in Pensacola on Wednesday that chairs and tents went flying and children held their hands over their ears.

video:video.video:title: DNA from bones on a Revolutionary War battlefield solves the case of 'America's oldest John Doe'
video:video.video:description: For more than two centuries, the Revolutionary War soldier was unknown and unnoticed. Now, thanks to happenstance and the wonders of DNA, he has a name, just in time for the 250th birthday of the nation he fought and died to create.

video:video.video:title: Rita Ora on playing Queen of Hearts in 'Descendants'
video:video.video:description: Rita Ora says she connected with Brandy "on another level" while making "Descendants: Wicked Wonderland."

video:video.video:title: World Cup beer sales are hopping. Brewers hope the stout demand outlasts the soccer tournament
video:video.video:description: The World Cup has been a bonanza for beer. Bars in Boston needed emergency deliveries after some taps ran dry. (AP Video by Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: California leads 12-state bid to freeze Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery deal
video:video.video:description: Twelve states challenged Paramount’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery on Monday, filing a lawsuit that argues the mega merger would “extinguish competition” in Hollywood and threaten jobs across the industry.

video:video.video:title: Trump hails 'tremendous love,' unity at NATO summit
video:video.video:description: A day after blasting allies for failing to support the U.S. in its war with Iran, President Donald Trump hailed the NATO summit in Turkey as "very successful" and filled with "tremendous love," praising the alliance's unity as he departed the two-day event.

video:video.video:title: Fans gather outside Madison Square Garden for Taylor Swift's wedding
video:video.video:description: "It's our version of a royal wedding." Taylor Swift fans gather outside Madison Square Garden for the pop megastar's marriage to Travis Kelce.

video:video.video:title: Grieving relatives and friends of Bangkok fire victims express sorrow and anger
video:video.video:description: Relatives and friends of the victims of a huge fire at a Bangkok music bar arrived at the Institute of Forensic Medicine on Tuesday to receive the bodies of the deceased.

video:video.video:title: Food writer Anissa Helou lifts the lid on her culinary inspirations
video:video.video:description: Before becoming one of the Middle East’s most acclaimed cooks and food writers, Anissa Helou had no intention of either path.

video:video.video:title: From Chocolate Avenue to the World Cup, how Hershey, Pennsylvania, shaped Christian Pulisic
video:video.video:description: Standout U.S. national soccer team player Christian Pulisic hails from Hershey, Pennsylvania. Known as the “Sweetest Place on Earth” thanks to its chocolate-drenched origins.

video:video.video:title: Iran mourners honor late supreme leader in seminary city of Qom
video:video.video:description: Iranian state television early Tuesday aired live images of tens of thousands of people walking toward Jamkaran Mosque, just south of Qom, for a funeral service for Khamenei.

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr celebrates 86th birthday
video:video.video:description: Ringo Starr was joined by his wife, Barbara Bach, and friends in Beverly Hills on Tuesday to celebrate his 86th birthday.

video:video.video:title: Marines conduct verification boarding on M/T Wen Yao in the Gulf of Oman, says US Central Command
video:video.video:description: American forces have redirected three commercial vessels attempting to breach the blockade, disabled one vessel that did not comply, and boarded another to ensure full adherence to the ongoing U.S. naval blockade against Iran.

video:video.video:title: Low testosterone testing explained
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Wednesday that he is rolling out a new screening program for “testosterone deficiency” among troops.

video:video.video:title: Protests as far-right Alternative for Germany holds convention, eyeing gains in regional elections
video:video.video:description: Thousands of protesters gathered on Saturday as Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) held its national convention in Erfurt.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, July 10th: Trump ousts members of bipartisan election commission; testimony concludes in Charlie Kirk assassination case; scrutiny grows over fatal ICE shooting in Houston; Spain wildfire kills more than a dozen people.

video:video.video:title: Landslides in Bangladesh kill at least eight Rohingya refugees
video:video.video:description: Landslides triggered by heavy monsoon rains in southeastern Bangladesh killed at least eight Rohingya refugees, including five children, in Cox’s Bazar.

video:video.video:title: Can AI change lives for the better?
video:video.video:description: AI has been dominating news headlines in recent years as people digest what the developments of large language models will mean for the future.

video:video.video:description: How far would you go for sports? We asked fans at Fanatics Fest to share their most unforgettable fan stories.

video:video.video:description: CCTV footage from the Ukrainian city of Sumy captured the moment visitors at a local coffee shop scrambled for cover during a Russian air strike.

video:video.video:title: Jagger and Wood hope for England World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Mick Jagger will be in Miami supporting England vs Norway on Saturday at the World Cup; Ronnie Wood has manager Thomas Tuchel on speed dial. (July 9)

video:video.video:title: Survivors and victims' relatives of deadly Bangkok bar fire seek compensation, gather belongings
video:video.video:description: The cause of the fire at the Rong Beer Na Ladprao bar is still under investigation. More than 70 people were injured and 30 people remain hospitalized, with 15 of them in intensive care units, according to Bangkok's Erawan emergency services center.

video:video.video:title: 'Operation Hard Ball' nets 24 arrests targeting international India-based organized crime gangs
video:video.video:description: Law enforcement officials at federal, local, and international agencies charged 37 alleged members of India-based transnational organized crime groups with multiple criminal acts, including the assassination in Canada of a prominent Indian activist.

video:video.video:title: Trump describes Iran as weakened but alludes to ongoing security concerns
video:video.video:description: "I'm their number one target." Trump told reporters on Wednesday before answering questions about security concerns with flying home aboard newly gifted Air Force One plane.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday he called FIFA President Gianni Infantino to review the red card given to U.S. men's soccer striker Folarin Balogun, calling it a "horrible" call.

video:video.video:title: What are earthquakes and why do they happen?
video:video.video:description: Earthquakes typically occur along edges of tectonic plates. But their impacts may be felt in a broader region. (AP video produced by Javier Arciga)

video:video.video:title: Bastille Day in Paris puts European military might centre stage
video:video.video:description: It acknowledges the day Parisians stormed the Bastille fortress and prison in 1789, helping spark the French Revolution that overthrew the monarchy.

video:video.video:description: Eight men were indicted on murder and terrorism conspiracy charges Thursday for their alleged roles in a thwarted drone and sniper attack on the UFC cage-fighting show staged at the White House in June.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump used a primetime address to the nation Thursday to elevate his yearslong push to raise doubts about the legitimacy of U.S. elections and dispute his 2020 loss — this time, to justify his push to pass a strict voter ID bill.

video:video.video:description: Residents in Texas assess flood waters in neighborhood streets, many impassable, after days of pounding rain.

video:video.video:title: Meteorologists warn of week of dangerous US temperatures: 'Heat is not to be played with'
video:video.video:description: Most of America's Lower 48 states are about to swelter under an unusually large, strong and long-lasting heat dome that will spike temperatures.

video:video.video:title: Attorney says autopsy of 1-year-old shot by Mississippi police conflicts with officers' account
video:video.video:description: A Mississippi family whose 1-year-old child was killed when officers fired on a car are challenging police claims about the shooting.

video:video.video:title: Marine heat wave off California coast leaves seabirds starving or dead
video:video.video:description: Many seabirds are starving to death as a marine heat wave lingers off California and fish seek deeper, cooler waters, scientists say.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. will lift sanctions on Turkey and consider restoring the sale of F-35 fighter jets to the country, signaling a possible reversal of a ban his administration imposed after Ankara bought Russian missile defense systems.

video:video.video:title: Russia's missile and drone attack on Ukraine kills at least 18
video:video.video:description: All of the ballistic missiles launched by Russia struck their targets, underscoring Kyiv’s need for more Patriot interceptor missiles — a point Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will likely reiterate at a NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, this week.

video:video.video:description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott has requested a Presidential Disaster Declaration to aid in the state's response to severe weather and flooding affecting communities statewide.

video:video.video:title: 1 dead and 3 missing after boat carrying mostly family members sinks near Alcatraz Island
video:video.video:description: One person died, three people were missing and 16 were rescued from the waters off San Francisco after a boat sank Tuesday afternoon while carrying mostly family members as part of a memorial service, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfire in Almeria that claimed at least 11 lives, 19 others are missing
video:video.video:description: A wildfire in southern Spain has killed at least 11 people, making it one of the country's deadliest on record, as soaring temperatures grip much of the country, authorities said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Trump ousts bipartisan election commissioners in latest push to reshape US voting
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has ousted members of a bipartisan federal election commission charged with assisting state and local election officials by distributing grants, testing voting systems, and maintaining national voter registration forms.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge in what Trump called Reflecting Pool vandalism
video:video.video:description: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge in what Trump called Reflecting Pool vandalism

video:video.video:title: Summer swimming returns to Paris with 3 bathing sites opened on the Seine
video:video.video:description: Swimming in the Seine is settling into a new Paris summer ritual as the city opened three supervised bathing sites on Saturday for the second year in a row.

video:video.video:title: Mahmoud Khalil files suit alleging a 'public-private' conspiracy to target Israel's critics
video:video.video:description: Khalil was joined by his lawyers, fellow advocates and supporters at a press briefing in New York Tuesday where they took turns at the podium to discuss the lawsuit and their ongoing fight to continue speaking up for Palestinian rights.

video:video.video:title: Billions for DHS, $20 million for body cameras yet officers in Houston shooting didn't have them
video:video.video:description: Democratic congresswoman Sylvia Garcia said that ICE Director David Venturella promised her that the agency will have body cameras on all officers in the field by the end of the month.

video:video.video:title: Partial touch and movement returns to paralysed man following brain implant
video:video.video:description: A man completely paralysed in a diving accident has been given a lasting sensation of touch and partial movement after a double neural bypass operation.

video:video.video:title: A dog's dinner: Hong Kong allows dogs to eat out at restaurants
video:video.video:description: It's a very special dinner time for dogs and their owners in Hong Kong. For the first time in more than 30 years they can go out together to restaurants, thanks to a new policy aimed at promoting the pet economy.

video:video.video:title: Widespread flooding as heavy storms hit Russia’s Ural
video:video.video:description: Torrential rain and thunderstorms have gripped the region since the beginning of summer, and forecasters say the same weather is expected to continue in the coming days.

video:video.video:title: Maher jokes White House called his Mark Twain Prize "fake news"
video:video.video:description: Before accepting the Mark Twain Prize, Bill Maher poked fun at the White House, which had labeled news of his selection for the honor as "fake news" when it was announced.

video:video.video:title: An empty middle seat is the latest perk United Airlines will offer on some flights
video:video.video:description: United Airlines is trying to attract flyers with another luxurious perk: an empty middle seat. They announced plans this week to roll out a new row of its “Economy Plus” seats with no neighbor in the middle spot — and instead use the space for a shared table

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 9
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, July 9th: U.S. says dozens of targets hit in Iran; Trump switches planes while returning to U.S.; Protest in Houston after ICE officer fatally shoots man; Graham Platner leaves Maine senate race.

video:video.video:title: Women amputees in South America train to be World Cup soccer contenders
video:video.video:description: Women amputees from several countries are training in Bolivia for the 2027 Women's Amputee Football World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in southwest Pakistan, killing 40 passengers
video:video.video:description: A passenger bus plunged from a highway into a rocky ravine in southwestern Pakistan early Friday, killing 40 people and injuring eight others in one of the deadliest road accidents in recent years, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Joy in Oyo as rescued students are reunited with their relatives after months in captivity
video:video.video:description: Scenes of joy unfolded in Oyo, Nigeria, as rescued students reunited with their relatives after months in captivity.

video:video.video:title: Retrofitted Qatari jet takes flight as Air Force One for Trump's trip to North Dakota
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday took his maiden voyage on a new Air Force One — a retrofitted Boeing 747-800 worth $400 million gifted by Qatar.

video:video.video:title: New York bar owner calls street closures and added security for Swift-Kelce wedding ‘ridiculous’
video:video.video:description: Co-owner of O'Briens Bar and Grill located across from Madison Square Garden questioned if the couple can afford the permits to close the streets and a “big lavish ceremony” then why not also buy out the nearby businesses so they are not adversely affected.

video:video.video:title: As audiences rediscover The Odyssey, Greek classrooms keep the epic alive
video:video.video:description: As the world prepares for a Hollywood adaptation of The Odyssey, Greek students continue a tradition that has endured for centuries.

video:video.video:title: US launches new strikes on Iran, revokes oil sales permit after 3 ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military attacked Iran early Wednesday after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz, part of an American effort that also revoked the Islamic Republic's ability to openly sell crude oil in the world market.

video:video.video:title: Philly youth soccer clubs hope that World Cup excitement leads to lasting support
video:video.video:description: Philadelphia youth soccer players got an opportunity to experience the World Cup up close. Now families and coaches hope the tournament's excitement brings lasting support for their under-resourced neighborhoods.

video:video.video:title: Virtual taekwondo set to take center stage at Asian Games
video:video.video:description: Virtual taekwondo is kicking its way into the mainstream as its popularity grows across Southeast Asia.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfires in Ukraine's Chernobyl exclusion zone
video:video.video:description: The Ukraine State Emergency Service said radiation levels in the exclusion zone remained within normal limits.

video:video.video:title: Alex Cameron jokes about growing anxiety over artificial intelligence
video:video.video:description: Ahead of the release of his upcoming album, "Late to Set," Australian recording artist Alex Cameron spoke with The Associated Press about growing anxiety over artificial intelligence and how it informs his music.

video:video.video:title: Fireworks display in Philadelphia marks 250th anniversary of US independence, following weather delay
video:video.video:description: Crowds gathered in Philadelphia early Sunday to watch the fireworks celebrating the 250th anniversary of American independence.

video:video.video:title: UK urges FIFA to investigate Argentina over Falklands banner at World Cup
video:video.video:description: During post-match celebrations, Argentine players held a banner handed over by fans in the stands, reading “Las Malvinas son Argentinas” – “The Malvinas are Argentine.”

video:video.video:title: Extreme heat shuts down the Great American State Fair in DC for a few hours
video:video.video:description: Extreme heat in the U.S. is impacting festivities commemorating the 250th anniversary of American independence, including a temporary closure of the Great American State Fair in Washington D.C.

video:video.video:title: Emergency services say number of injured in Russian airstrike in Zaporizhzhia has risen to 29
video:video.video:description: The number of people injured in a Russian airstrike on Zaporizhzhia rose to 29, including two children, Ukraine's State Emergency Service said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Storm surge slams into windows as Typhoon Bavi hits China's Taizhou city
video:video.video:description: A storm surge slammed against windows as Typhoon Bavi barreled into China's eastern coast, bringing heavy rain and huge waves to Zhejiang province late Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Thursday marks 1,000 days since Hamas-led attack on Israel sparked war in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and people in Israel are marking 1,000 days of war. It's the 1,000th day since Hamas militants carried out their attack in Israel on October 7, 2023.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong bookseller Lam Wing-kee dies in Taipei, aged 70
video:video.video:description: Lam Wing-kee, a former bookseller in Hong Kong who became a symbol of resistance to Beijing's crackdown on speech freedom after he was seized by Chinese authorities in late 2015, has died in Taiwan, the island's official Central News Agency reported, citing an unnamed source.

video:video.video:title: Robotic elephants draw crowds and controversy in some of India's Hindu temples
video:video.video:description: In southern India, robotic elephants are replacing live ones in Hindu rituals and festivals following concerns over animal abuse and fatal elephant rampages.
video:video.video:tag: People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals

video:video.video:title: Spain en route to quarterfinals after ending Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup career
video:video.video:description: Mikel Merino scored in the first minute of second-half stoppage time, and Spain beat Portugal 1-0 on Monday to end the World Cup career of superstar Cristiano Ronaldo.

video:video.video:title: 'The Odyssey' stars talk about the 'overwhelming' adventure from Chris Nolan
video:video.video:description: Matt Damon, Anne Hathaway, Tom Holland and Zendaya discuss working with Christopher Nolan for his upcoming blockbuster film, "The Odyssey." (July 10)

video:video.video:title: British nationals who fled their homes in Spain due to wildfires describe their experience
video:video.video:description: British expats living in the area of Spain which was devastated by wildfires have spoken of panic as residents were told to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Sabrina Bryan 'never stopped wearing cheetah'
video:video.video:description: Sabrina Bryan says cheetah print never left her wardrobe. She will return to her iconic Disney role as Dorinda in "The Cheetah Girls: Next Gen."

video:video.video:title: Burnham declared leader of UK's Labour Party and will become prime minister on Monday
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham was officially declared leader of Britain’s governing Labour Party on Friday. The former mayor of Greater Manchester was the only contender in the center-left party’s leadership contest to replace departing Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who was forced out by a Labour rebellion.

video:video.video:title: Should you leave your air conditioner on all day?
video:video.video:description: Is it better to shut off your air conditioner when you leave, or keep it running? Experts say the answer isn’t one-size-fits-all. (AP Video by Kiki Sideris)

video:video.video:title: Spectacular orange lava flows from Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano
video:video.video:description: An eruption of Mount Etna, on the Italian island of Sicily, produced a spectacular lava show that turned part of Europe's largest active volcano orange.

video:video.video:title: What to know about Nigel Farage, Count Binface and the state of UK politics
video:video.video:description: Here’s what to know about the recent dramatic development in British politics involving Reform UK leader Nigel Farage...and now, Count Binface.

video:video.video:title: Heavy rains keep drenching South Texas, residents encouraged to seek higher ground
video:video.video:description: Homes in Boerne, Texas were surrounded by heavily flooded streets as city officials said they have shut down roads.
